title: Temporal filter: use start/end date range for year-specific queries
state_description: Question asks about an entity's role, team, or position at a specific year (e.g., 'in 2012', 'in 2013', 'in 1980').

action.description: Use FILTER with start and end date predicates to check that the target year falls within the valid range of a relationship (e.g., roster tenure, political term).
steps: Identify the time-range predicates in the subgraph (e.g., from/to, start/end)
Use FILTER (?start <= YEAR && ?end >= YEAR) or FILTER (?from <= DATE && ?to >= DATE)
Use appropriate XSD types: xsd:gYear for year-only values, xsd:date for full dates
OPTIONAL the end date to handle ongoing/current positions with no end date

avoid: Do not use string comparisons or skip temporal filtering when a year is specified.

title: ORDER BY must be outside WHERE block in SPARQL
state_description: SPARQL query fails with syntax error at 'ORDER' or 'LIMIT' when placed inside the WHERE clause.

action.description: Move ORDER BY and LIMIT clauses outside the closing brace of the WHERE block.
steps: Close the WHERE block with } before writing ORDER BY
Place ORDER BY clause after WHERE: WHERE { ... } ORDER BY ...
Place LIMIT clause after ORDER BY: WHERE { ... } ORDER BY ... LIMIT N

avoid: Do not put ORDER BY, LIMIT, OFFSET, GROUP BY, or HAVING inside the WHERE { } block.
